comment.body: UAE banks have begun mass account closures of Russian companies, with 20% to 30% facing audits, operational restrictions, or outright closures, as the country, hosting over 4,000 Russian firms, tightens payment controls amid US sanctions pressure, RBC reported, citing lawyers and consultants. Accounts may be closed for minor issues, like missing a bank call or delayed responses, with banks sometimes demanding up to 20 certified documents within 24 hours, said Maria Chumanova, managing partner at FTL Advisers.

comment.body:
Alma, i get why you ask that, but I think calling it a bull trap might be more about being afraid of a reversal than what the price is actually showing. If we call every move a trap too soon, we just might be guessing instead of watching how things play out. 

in terms of you asking what real bullish momentum is well, it shows up when buyers keep stepping in, holding higher lows, and pushing through key levels. until that breaks down, i would probably treat it as legit. Calling it a trap without real proof might just be trying to outsmart the market instead of respecting it

comment.body: I look at it more as what would break the structure of the market. So for example, Kurtosis is elevated and skew is moving more positive so it shows a propensity for right tailed events. But if you look at entropy in the market its pretty low which you could infer as the market is moving in a predictable manner which tells you that right now we're still pretty one-sided. So any small shock breaks the structure which would then look like a bulltrap
comment.raw_body: I look at it more as what would break the structure of the market.
